title: senecio perralderianus subsp. perralderianus
intro: Senecio perralderianus subsp. perralderianus, also known as Perralder's ragwort, is a plant species in the dandelion family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being a bitter tonic, digestive aid, and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and possibly aiding in the treatment of certain respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Senecio perralderianus subsp. perralderianus can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support the body's natural processes and promote overall well-being.
